appliedUpdates.description: Added note about unbounded unfiltered_commands table growth and potential need for pruning
Added os.homedir() mocking pattern for config/settings test isolation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that the 'State via closure variables, NOT classes' convention applies to extension-level state management in index.ts. Pi Component implementations (settings panels) must use classes to satisfy the Component interface (render/handleInput/invalidate), consistent with pi-lcm and pi-voice reference implementations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: settings-panel feature uses 'export class RtkSettingsPanel', same pattern as pi-lcm LcmSettingsPanel and pi-voice VoiceSettingsPanel. Reviewer flagged the ambiguity.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The pi-rtk-worker skill says 'Do NOT read IMPLEMENTATION.md' but feature descriptions reference it ('per IMPLEMENTATION.md'). Workers naturally read it when the feature description points to it. Consider either removing the prohibition or rewording feature descriptions to be self-contained.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: gain-command worker read IMPLEMENTATION.md early in the session despite the skill prohibition. The feature description says 'per IMPLEMENTATION.md' which implies reading it. Same pattern likely repeated in discover-command and settings-panel features.

rejectedObservations.observation: periodToCutoff() is duplicated in src/tracker.ts and src/gain.ts
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking code quality issue. Both implementations are correct and in sync. Too minor for guidance — normal refactoring candidate.

rejectedObservations.observation: Subcommand routing should be designed for extensibility from the start
rejectedObservations.reason: Already resolved — current HEAD has all subcommands (gain, discover, settings) properly routed. The gain-command commit naturally only had one subcommand.
Project-wide design choice, not specific to any feature. Enabling it could cause widespread breakage and is a deliberate tradeoff.
